sections.label: Section 1375
sections.translation: who bears the semblance of a friend , fence the snares of ruin too high to be overleaped ? This is the contest of an ancient feud , pondered by me of old , and it has come , however long delayed . I stand where I dealt the blow ; my purpose is achieved .

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: οὕτω δ ἔπραξα , καὶ τάδ οὐκ ἀρνήσομαι :
sections.translation: Thus have I done the deed ; deny it I will not . Round him , as if to catch a haul of fish , I cast an impassable net — fatal wealth of robe — so that he should neither escape nor ward off doom . Twice I struck him , and with two groans

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.translation: his limbs relaxed . Once he had fallen , I dealt him yet a third stroke to grace my prayer to the infernal Zeus , the savior of the dead . Fallen thus , he gasped away his life , and as he breathed forth quick spurts of blood ,

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: βάλλει μ ἐρεμνῇ ψακάδι φοινίας δρόσου ,
sections.translation:
he struck me with dark drops of gory dew ; while I rejoiced no less than the sown earth is gladdened in heaven ’ s refreshing rain at the birthtime of the flower buds .
Since then the case stands thus , old men of Argos , rejoice , if you would rejoice ; as for me , I glory in the deed .

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: εἰ δ ἦν πρεπόντων ὥστ ἐπισπένδειν νεκρῷ ,
sections.translation: And had it been a fitting act to pour libations on the corpse , over him this would have been done justly , more than justly . With so many accursed lies has he filled the mixing-bowl in his own house , and now he has come home and himself drained it to the dregs .

sections.label: Section 1399
sections.translation: We are shocked at your tongue , how bold-mouthed you are ,

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: ἥτις τοιόνδ ἐπ ἀνδρὶ κομπάζεις λόγον .
sections.translation: that over your husband you can utter such a boastful speech .

sections.label: Section 1401
sections.translation: You are testing me as if I were a witless woman . But my heart does not quail , and I say to you who know it well — and whether you wish to praise or to blame me , it is all one — here is Agamemnon ,

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: πόσις , νεκρὸς δέ , τῆσδε δεξιᾶς χερὸς
sections.translation: my husband , now a corpse , the work of this right hand , a just workman . So stands the case .

sections.label: Section 1.1407
sections.original: τί κακόν , ὦ γύναι , χθονοτρεφὲς ἐδανὸν
sections.translation: Woman , what poisonous herb nourished by the earth have you tasted , what potion drawn from the flowing sea , that you have taken upon yourself this maddened rage and the loud curses voiced by the public ?

sections.label: Section 1.1410
sections.original: ἀπέδικες ἀπέταμές τ : [*] ἀπόπολις [*] δ ἔσει
sections.translation: You have cast him off ; you have cut him off ; and out from the land shall you be cast , a burden of hatred to your people .

sections.label: Section 1.1412
sections.original: νῦν μὲν δικάζεις ἐκ πόλεως φυγὴν ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: It ’ s now that you would doom me to exile from the land , to the hatred of my people and the execration of the public voice ; though then you had nothing to urge against him that lies here . And yet he ,

sections.label: Section 1.1415
sections.original: ὃς οὐ προτιμῶν , ὡσπερεὶ βοτοῦ μόρον ,
sections.translation: valuing no more than if it had been a beast that perished — though sheep were plenty in his fleecy folds — he sacrificed his own child , she whom I bore with dearest travail , to charm the blasts of Thrace . Is it not he whom you should have banished from this land

sections.label: Section 1.1420
sections.translation: in requital for his polluting deed ? No ! When you arraign what I have done , you are a stern judge . Well , I warn you : threaten me thus on the understanding that I am prepared , conditions equal , to let you lord it over me if you shall vanquish me by force . But if a god shall bring the contrary to pass ,

sections.label: Section 1.1425
sections.original: γνώσει διδαχθεὶς ὀψὲ γοῦν τὸ σωφρονεῖν .
sections.translation: you shall learn discretion though taught the lesson late .

sections.label: Section 1.1426
sections.translation: You are proud of spirit , and your speech is overbearing . Even as your mind is maddened by your deed of blood , upon your face a stain of blood shows full plain to behold . Bereft of all honor , forsaken of your friends ,

sections.label: Section 1.1430
sections.translation: you shall hereafter atone for stroke with stroke ,

sections.label: Section 1431
sections.original: καὶ τήνδ ἀκούεις ὁρκίων ἐμῶν θέμιν :
sections.translation: Listen then to this too , this the righteous sanction on my oath : by Justice , exacted for my child , by Ate , by the Avenging Spirit , to whom I sacrificed that man , hope does not tread for me the halls of fear ,

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: ἕως ἂν αἴθῃ πῦρ ἐφ ἑστίας ἐμῆς [*]
sections.translation: so long as the fire upon my hearth is kindled by Aegisthus , loyal in heart to me as in days gone by . For he is no slight shield of confidence to me . Here lies the man who did me wrong , plaything of each Chryseis at Ilium ;

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: ἥ τ αἰχμάλωτος ἥδε καὶ τερασκόπος
sections.translation: and here she lies , his captive , and auguress , and concubine , his oracular faithful whore , yet equally familiar with the seamen ’ s benches . The pair has met no undeserved fate . For he lies thus ; while she , who , like a swan ,

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.translation: has sung her last lament in death , lies here , his beloved ; but to me she has brought for my bed an added relish of delight .

sections.label: Section 1.1448
sections.original: φεῦ , τίς ἂν ἐν τάχει , μὴ περιώδυνος ,
sections.translation: Alas ! Ah that some fate , free from excess of suffering , nor yet with lingering bed of pain ,

sections.label: Section 1.1450
sections.original: μόλοι τὸν ἀεὶ φέρουσ ἐν ἡμῖν
sections.translation: might come full soon and bring to us everlasting and endless sleep , now that our most gracious guardian has been laid low , who in a woman ’ s cause had much endured and by a woman ’ s hand has lost his life .

sections.label: Section 1.1455
sections.translation: O mad Helen , who did yourself alone destroy these many lives , these lives exceeding many , beneath the walls of Troy . Now you have bedecked yourself with your final crown , that shall long last in memory ,

sections.label: Section 1.1460
sections.translation: because of blood not to be washed away . Truly in those days strife , an affliction that has subdued its lord , dwelt in the house .

sections.label: Section 1462
sections.translation: Do not burden yourself with thoughts such as these , nor invoke upon yourself the fate of death . Nor yet turn your wrath upon Helen ,

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.translation: and deem her a slayer of men , as if she alone had destroyed many a Danaan life and had wrought anguish past all cure .

sections.label: Section 1.1468
sections.translation: O Fiend who falls upon this house and Tantalus ’ two descendants , [*]

sections.label: Section 1.1470
sections.translation: you who by the hands of women exert a rule matching their temper , a rule bitter to my soul ! Perched over his body like a hateful raven , in hoarse notes she chants her song of triumph .

sections.label: Section 1475
sections.translation: Now you have corrected the judgment of your lips in that you name the thrice-gorged Fiend of this race . For by him the lust for lapping blood is fostered in the mouth ; so before

sections.label: Section 1480
sections.translation: the ancient wound is healed , fresh blood is spilled .

sections.label: Section 2.1481
sections.translation: Truly you speak of a mighty Fiend , haunting the house , and heavy in his wrath ( alas , alas !)— an evil tale of catastrophic fate insatiate ;

sections.label: Section 2.1485
sections.translation: woe , woe , done by will of Zeus , author of all , worker of all ! For what is brought to pass for mortal men save by will of Zeus ? What herein is not wrought of god ?

sections.label: Section 2.1489
sections.translation: Alas , alas , my King , my King ,

sections.label: Section 2.1490
sections.translation: how shall I bewail you ? How voice my heartfelt love for you ? To lie in this spider ’ s web , breathing forth your life in an impious death ! Ah me , to lie on this ignoble bed , struck down in treacherous death wrought

sections.label: Section 2.1495
sections.translation: by a weapon of double edge wielded by the hand of your own wife !

sections.label: Section 1497
sections.translation: Do you affirm this deed is mine ? Do not imagine that I am Agamemnon ’ s spouse .

sections.label: Section 1500
sections.translation: A phantom resembling that corpse ’ s wife , the ancient bitter evil spirit of Atreus , that grim banqueter , has offered him in payment , sacrificing a full-grown victim in vengeance for those slain babes .

sections.label: Section 2.1505
sections.translation: That you are innocent of this murder — who will bear you witness ? How could anyone do so ? And yet the evil genius of his father might well be your accomplice . By force

sections.label: Section 2.1510
sections.translation: amid streams of kindred blood black Havoc presses on to where he shall grant vengeance for the gore of children served for meat .

sections.label: Section 2.1513
sections.translation: Alas , alas , my King , my King , how shall I bewail you ?

sections.label: Section 2.1515
sections.original: φρενὸς ἐκ φιλίας τί ποτ εἴπω ;
sections.translation: How voice my heartfelt love for you ? To lie in this spider ’ s web , breathing forth your life in impious death ! Alas , to lie on this ignoble bed , struck down in treacherous death

sections.label: Section 2.1520
sections.translation: wrought by a weapon of double edge wielded by your own wife ’ s hand !

sections.label: Section 1521
sections.translation: Neither do I think he met an ignoble death . And did he not himself by treachery bring ruin on his house ?

sections.label: Section 1525
sections.original: ἀλλ ἐμὸν ἐκ τοῦδ ἔρνος ἀερθέν ,
sections.translation: Yet , as he has suffered — worthy prize of worthy deed — for what he did to my sweet flower , shoot sprung from him , the sore-wept Iphigenia , let him make no great boasts in the halls of Hades , since with death dealt him by the sword he has paid for what he first began .

sections.label: Section 3.1530
sections.translation: Bereft of any ready expedient of thought , I am bewildered where to turn now that the house is tottering . I fear the beating storm of bloody rain that shakes the house ; no longer does it descend in drops .

sections.label: Section 3.1535
sections.original: δίκην [*] δ ἐπ ἄλλο πρᾶγμα θηγάνει [*] βλάβης
sections.translation: Yet on other whetstones Destiny is sharpening justice for another evil deed .

sections.label: Section 3.1537
sections.original: ἰὼ γᾶ γᾶ , εἴθ ἔμ ἐδέξω ,
sections.translation: O Earth , Earth , if only you had taken me to yourself before ever I had lived to see my lord

sections.label: Section 3.1540
sections.translation: occupying a lowly bed of a silver-sided bath ! Who shall bury him ? Who shall lament him ? Will you harden your heart to do this — you who have slain your own husband — to lament for him

sections.label: Section 3.1545
sections.original: ψυχῇ τ [*] ἄχαριν χάριν ἀντ ἔργων
sections.translation: and crown your unholy work with an uncharitable gift to his spirit , atoning for your monstrous deeds ? And who , as with tears he utters praise over the hero ’ s grave ,

sections.label: Section 3.1550
sections.translation: shall sorrow in sincerity of heart ?

sections.label: Section 1551
sections.original: οὐ σὲ προσήκει τὸ μέλημ ἀλέγειν [*] [*]
sections.translation: To care for that duty is no concern of yours . By our hands down he fell , down to death , and down below shall we bury him — but not with wailings from his household .

sections.label: Section 1555
sections.translation: No ! Iphigenia , his daughter , as is due , shall meet her father lovingly at the swift-flowing ford of sorrows , and shall fling her arms around him and kiss him .

sections.label: Section 3.1560
sections.translation: Reproach thus meets reproach in turn — hard is the struggle to decide . The spoiler is despoiled , the slayer pays penalty . Yet , while Zeus remains on his throne , it remains true that to him who does it shall be done ; for it is law .

sections.label: Section 3.1565
sections.original: τίς ἂν γονὰν ἀραῖον [*] ἐκβάλοι δόμων ;
sections.translation: Who can cast from out the house the seed of the curse ? The race is bound fast in calamity .

sections.label: Section 1567
sections.translation: Upon this divine deliverance have you rightly touched . As for me , however , I am willing to make a sworn compact with the Fiend of the house of Pleisthenes [*]

sections.label: Section 1570
sections.translation: that I will be content with what is done , hard to endure though it is . Henceforth he shall leave this house and bring tribulation upon some other race by murder of kin . A small part of the wealth is fully enough for me , if I may but rid these halls

sections.label: Section 1575
sections.original: βαιὸν ἐχούσῃ πᾶν ἀπόχρη μοι [*] μανίας μελάθρων
sections.translation: of the frenzy of mutual murder .

sections.label: Section 1577
sections.translation: Hail gracious light of the day of retribution ! At last the hour has come when I can say that the gods who avenge mortal men look down from on high upon the crimes of earth .

sections.label: Section 1580
sections.translation:
Now that , to my joy , I behold this man lying here in a robe spun by the Avenging Spirits and making full payment for the deeds contrived in craft by his father ’ s hand .
For Atreus , lord of this land , this man ’ s father , challenged in his sovereignty , drove forth , from city and from home , Thyestes , who ( to speak it clearly ) was my father
